discussion_posts.name: sriyshnav
discussion_posts.content: Doing DFS on every cell may seem promising but the time complexity then would be O((N*M)^2) which might be slow for the given constraint. Why not start from the end? why not DFS from the Atlantic and pacific coasts.

Also what is the goal, to get from any cell touching the Pacific to any cell touching the Atlantic in both directions?

discussion_posts.name: Adoni1203
discussion_posts.content: Hi,\n\nDid anyone solve this problem with DP? I find my output tend to miss one or two correct points and I don't know why

discussion_posts.name: sato
discussion_posts.content:
Steps to solve 
1. Create a set of co-ordinates from where we can reach pacific ocean. lets call it P_Set. 
2. Create a set of co-ordinates from where we can reach atlantic ocean. lets call it A_Set
3. Our answer would be intersection of A_Set and P_Set

Calculate P_Set
1. Add all the boundary co-ordiates to the set along the pacific ocean
2. Perform BFS to add to the set if curr height > parent height.
